hypothesis: Incorporating explicit heating energy (via temperature-dependent heat capacities and furnace efficiency) and product isolation costs (via ML-predicted solubilities/volatilities of byproducts) into the analysis module will reverse the current economic ranking for Ca2SiO4, making the Ca3SiO5+SiO2 route more cost-effective than CaO+SiO2 for industrial batches ≥100 kg at electricity prices ≥0.15 €/kWh.
